verses.text: Moses, keeping sheep at Horeb, sees a bush burning yet not consumed, and turns aside. Strange sight. God arrests him with a wonder.
Moses, keeping sheep at Horeb, sees a bush burning yet not consumed, and turns aside. Strange sight. God arrests him with a wonder.
Moses, keeping sheep at Horeb, sees a bush burning yet not consumed, and turns aside. Strange sight. God arrests him with a wonder.
God calls, 'put off thy shoes,' for 'I am the God of thy father'; Moses hides his face. Holy ground. God's presence demands reverence.
God calls, 'put off thy shoes,' for 'I am the God of thy father'; Moses hides his face. Holy ground. God's presence demands reverence.
God calls, 'put off thy shoes,' for 'I am the God of thy father'; Moses hides his face. Holy ground. God's presence demands reverence.
'I have surely seen the affliction of my people... and am come down to deliver them.' God sees. He is moved by His people's suffering.
'I have surely seen the affliction of my people... and am come down to deliver them.' God sees. He is moved by His people's suffering.
'I have surely seen the affliction of my people... and am come down to deliver them.' God sees. He is moved by His people's suffering.
He sends Moses to Pharaoh; Moses asks, 'Who am I?' but God promises, 'Certainly I will be with thee.' His presence. The call rests not on Moses but on God.
He sends Moses to Pharaoh; Moses asks, 'Who am I?' but God promises, 'Certainly I will be with thee.' His presence. The call rests not on Moses but on God.
He sends Moses to Pharaoh; Moses asks, 'Who am I?' but God promises, 'Certainly I will be with thee.' His presence. The call rests not on Moses but on God.
Moses asks God's name; 'I AM THAT I AM... this is my name for ever.' The eternal. God is the self-existent, unchanging One.
Moses asks God's name; 'I AM THAT I AM... this is my name for ever.' The eternal. God is the self-existent, unchanging One.
Moses asks God's name; 'I AM THAT I AM... this is my name for ever.' The eternal. God is the self-existent, unchanging One.
He bids Moses gather the elders and ask Pharaoh for a three days' journey to sacrifice. The plan. God lays out His step-by-step deliverance.
He bids Moses gather the elders and ask Pharaoh for a three days' journey to sacrifice. The plan. God lays out His step-by-step deliverance.
He bids Moses gather the elders and ask Pharaoh for a three days' journey to sacrifice. The plan. God lays out His step-by-step deliverance.
Pharaoh will not yield but to 'a mighty hand'; Israel shall 'spoil the Egyptians.' Forewarned. God foretells both the struggle and the triumph.
